input.prompt:
Thoroughness: very thorough.

Task: Read and inspect the repository at /Users/user/Desktop/langsmith-cli broadly enough to understand its architecture. The user asked to "read every file in this repo and write a one-paragraph summary of the architecture." Since this is a read-only architecture summary task, inspect all relevant tracked source/config/docs/test files and note any generated/vendor/ignored directories you skip if applicable. Return exactly one concise paragraph summarizing the architecture: primary language/framework, package/module layout, CLI entry points, major subsystems, testing/build tooling, and how the pieces fit together. Do not modify files.

content.text: langsmith-cli is a Go 1.25 Cobra CLI with `cmd/langsmith/main.go` delegating to `internal/cmd.NewRootCmd`; it wraps LangSmith APIs through `internal/client`, resolves auth and profiles through `internal/config` and `internal/cmdutil`, and exposes commands for projects, traces, runs, threads, datasets, examples, evaluators, experiments, hub/prompt/fleet resources, sandboxes, insights, issues, auth/profile/workspace, self-update, trace setup, and an OpenAPI-driven `api` subcommand. Shared packages handle filters, data extraction, structured command behavior, and table/tree/JSON output, while tests are colocated Go tests with optional integration tags, CI runs vet/test/build/lint and install smoke tests, and releases use GoReleaser static cross-platform binaries.
